result.stdout:
dotnet-certes

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] [command]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  account  Manage ACME accounts.
  az       Deploy certificates to Microsoft Azure.
  cert     Export certificates.
  order    Manage certificate orders.
  server   Manage ACME servers.

commands.payload:
dotnet-certes

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] [command]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  account  Manage ACME accounts.
  az       Deploy certificates to Microsoft Azure.
  cert     Export certificates.
  order    Manage certificate orders.
  server   Manage ACME servers.

result.stdout:
account
  Manage ACME accounts.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] account [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  new <email>     Create new ACME account.
  set <key-path>  Set the default ACME account.
  show            Get the ACME account.
  update <email>  Update ACME account.

result.stdout:
new
  Create new ACME account.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] account new <email>

Arguments:
  <email>  The E-mail for ACME account.

Options:
  --out, --out-path <out-path>      Path to save the generated key.
  -s, --server <server>             The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>  Path to the ACME account key.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
az
  Deploy certificates to Microsoft Azure.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] az [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  app <order-id> <domain> <app>  Deploy certificates to Azure app services.
  dns <order-id> <domain>        Configure Azure DNS to respone to ACME DNS challenge.
  set                            Configure default credentials for Microsoft Azure.

commands.payload:
az
  Deploy certificates to Microsoft Azure.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] az [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  app <order-id> <domain> <app>  Deploy certificates to Azure app services.
  dns <order-id> <domain>        Configure Azure DNS to respone to ACME DNS challenge.
  set                            Configure default credentials for Microsoft Azure.

result.stdout:
app
  Deploy certificates to Azure app services.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] az app <order-id> <domain> <app>

Arguments:
  <order-id>  The URI of the certificate order.
  <domain>    The domain.
  <app>       The Auzre app service name.

Options:
  --slot <slot>                        The Azure app service deployment slot.
  --private-key <private-key>          The private key for the certificate.
  --preferred-chain <preferred-chain>  Preferred root certificate.
  -s, --server <server>                The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>     Path to the ACME account key.
  --tenant-id <tenant-id>              The Azure tenant ID.
  --client-id <client-id>              The client ID of the Azure service principal.
  --client-secret <client-secret>      The client secret of the Azure service principal.
  --subscription-id <subscription-id>  The Azure subscription ID.
  --resource-group <resource-group>    The resource group.
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
app
  Deploy certificates to Azure app services.

  --client-secret <client-secret>      The client secret of the Azure service principal.
  --subscription-id <subscription-id>  The Azure subscription ID.
  --resource-group <resource-group>    The resource group.
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
pfx
  Export certificate to PEM for an order.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] cert pfx <--order-id> <--password>

Arguments:
  <--order-id>  The URI of the certificate order.
  <--password>  The password for the PFX.

Options:
  -s, --server <server>                The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>     Path to the ACME account key.
  --out, --out-path <out-path>         Path to save the certificate.
  --private-key <private-key>          The private key for the certificate.
  --friendly-name <friendly-name>      Friendly name for the certificate.
  --issuer <issuer>                    Additional certificates for building the certificate chain.
  --preferred-chain <preferred-chain>  Preferred root certificate.
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
pfx
  Export certificate to PEM for an order.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] cert pfx <--order-id> <--password>

Arguments:
  <--order-id>  The URI of the certificate order.
  <--password>  The password for the PFX.

Options:
  -s, --server <server>                The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>     Path to the ACME account key.
  --out, --out-path <out-path>         Path to save the certificate.
  --private-key <private-key>          The private key for the certificate.
  --friendly-name <friendly-name>      Friendly name for the certificate.
  --issuer <issuer>                    Additional certificates for building the certificate chain.
  --preferred-chain <preferred-chain>  Preferred root certificate.
  -?, -h, --help                       Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
order
  Manage certificate orders.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] order [command]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  authz <order-id> <domain> <challenge-type>     Get the details of an authorization.
  finalize <--order-id>                          Finalize an order.
  list                                           List all certificate orders.
  new <domains>                                  Create new certificate order.
  show <order-id>                                Get the details of a certificate order.
  validate <order-id> <domain> <challenge-type>  Validate the authorization challenge.

commands.payload:

  new <domains>                                  Create new certificate order.
  show <order-id>                                Get the details of a certificate order.
  validate <order-id> <domain> <challenge-type>  Validate the authorization challenge.

authz
  Get the details of an authorization.

Usage:

Arguments:
  <order-id>        The URI of the certificate order.
  <domain>          The domain.
  <challenge-type>  The challenge type, http or dns.

Options:
  -s, --server <server>             The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>  Path to the ACME account key.
  -?, -h, --help                    Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
finalize
  Finalize an order.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] order finalize <--order-id>

Arguments:
  <--order-id>  The URI of the certificate order.

Options:
  -s, --server <server>                      The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>           Path to the ACME account key.
  --dn <dn>                                  The distinguished name for the certificate request.
  --out, --out-path <out-path>               Path to save the generated key.
  --private-key <private-key>                The private key for the certificate.
  --key-algorithm <ES256|ES384|ES512|RS256>  The algorithm to the private key for the certificate. Supported values are RS256, ES256, ES384 and ES512. [default: ES256]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
finalize
  Finalize an order.

Usage:
  dotnet-certes [options] order finalize <--order-id>

Arguments:
  <--order-id>  The URI of the certificate order.

Options:
  -s, --server <server>                      The dictionary URI to an ACME server.
  -k, --key, --key-path <key-path>           Path to the ACME account key.
  --dn <dn>                                  The distinguished name for the certificate request.
  --out, --out-path <out-path>               Path to save the generated key.
  --private-key <private-key>                The private key for the certificate.
  --key-algorithm <ES256|ES384|ES512|RS256>  The algorithm to the private key for the certificate. Supported values are RS256, ES256, ES384 and ES512. [default: ES256]
  -?, -h, --help                             Show help and usage information
